$plural_rules: Tagalog: 2 categories (one, other) — but generally singular/plural distinction less marked than English

errors.permission: {TODO: native review — note: politeness register matters here}

$cultural_notes.honorifics: Tagalog uses honorifics (po/opo) for politeness. Citizen-facing surfaces should default to formal register; product-internal can be informal.
$cultural_notes.english_loanwords: Tagalog readily incorporates English tech terms ('I-save', 'I-cancel'). Stewards should preserve this idiom rather than over-translating.
$cultural_notes.code_switching: Taglish (Tagalog + English) is standard in tech-savvy Manila / Cebu audiences. The doctrine's tier-1 plain-language flag (Part 5 §20) decides per-surface.
